etymology.description: semantic 禾(禾, grain) + phonetic 厶(厶) -> private, selfish
etymology.interpretation: The character 私 (sī, "selfish; private") combines the semantic 禾(禾) (hé, "grain"), symbolizing the essential or personal aspect of life, with the phonetic component 厶(厶) (sī, "private"). The pairing implies something reserved or owned individually rather than publicly, hence 'private' or 'selfish'.

examples.chinese: 他总是把私事混为公事(他總是把私事混為公事)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì bǎ sīshì hùn wéi gōngshì.
examples.english: He always mixes private matters with official ones.

examples.chinese: 在工作中不应表现得太私(在工作中不應表現得太私)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gōngzuò zhōng bù yīng biǎoxiàn de tài sī.
examples.english: One shouldn't appear too selfish at work.

examples.chinese: 这封信是私信(這封信是私信)，请勿公开(請勿公開)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè fēng xìn shì sīxìn, qǐng wù gōngkāi.
examples.english: This is a private letter, please do not make it public.
